6.reason: The loop condition should check for -1 to terminate, not compare with -1; also the input may not end with EOF

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The loop condition should check for -1 to terminate, not compare with -1; also the input may not end with EOF
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The loop condition should stop when flag==-1, not when scanf returns -1. This causes an extra iteration that ignores the -1 operation, and if there were additional numbers after -1, they would be processed incorrectly. The proper condition is while(scanf("%d",&flag)==1 && flag!=-1).]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The loop never breaks when flag is -1; should break on flag==-1 to stop reading.

8.reason: [The stack capacity is 100, so push should be allowed when i<100, not i<99. This error effectively reduces usable capacity to 99, and also fails to produce an "error" when the stack is full (i==100).]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The stack capacity is 100, so push should be allowed when i<100, not i<99. This error effectively reduces usable capacity to 99, and also fails to produce an "error" when the stack is full (i==100).]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Condition i<99 is incorrect; should be i<100 to allow full stack capacity (indices 0-99).
